craig-shields-jtv.name: Craig Shields
craig-shields-jtv.title: Chief Digital Officer
craig-shields-jtv.fit: Agentic AI & workflow automation + predictive & generative ai
craig-shields-jtv.why: JTV runs one of the most operationally complex digital businesses in retail — live commerce, a large site, marketplaces, and retail media all producing signals faster than any team can read them. Your event profile points squarely at agentic AI and workflow automation. pts: At the C-level, the bottleneck isn’t dashboards — it’s the gap between what the data says and what the team does next. An AI layer is only as good as the measurement underneath it.
With marketplaces and retail media in the mix, channel-reported numbers disagree by design. MTA, MMM, and incrementality give you one defensible version of the truth to operate from.
Agentic AI evaluated at the executive level needs a governance answer: what does it monitor, what does it draft, and who approves. Moby is built around exactly that contract.

wf.t: Daily business pulse, written for the operator
wf.m: Moby monitors revenue, spend, contribution, and conversion across site, marketplaces, and channels every morning.
wf.d: It drafts a short plain-English brief: what changed, why it likely changed, and what’s worth a decision today.
wf.a: Your team approves any follow-up action — nothing moves without sign-off.

wf.t: Anomaly detection with explanations, not alerts
wf.m: Moby watches for breaks in trend — a channel’s efficiency dropping, a category stalling, a marketplace fee shift hitting margin.
wf.d: Instead of a red number, it drafts the explanation and a recommended response.
wf.a: You or the owning lead approves the response before anything is executed.

wf.t: One operating layer across growth, retention, and finance
wf.m: Custom dashboards unify paid media, retention, site, and P&L views so every team argues from the same numbers.
wf.d: Moby drafts the weekly operating review against those views — the prep work, done before the meeting.
wf.a: Leadership keeps the decisions; Moby keeps the legwork.

craig-shields-jtv.hook: Maxx’s session is about turning first-party data into profit with AI-powered CX — the same build-vs-buy question JTV is working through on agentic AI, told from the operator’s seat.

katherine-martinez-rent-the-runway.fit: Content marketing & SEO + product discovery & personalization
katherine-martinez-rent-the-runway.why: Rent The Runway operates as an apparel and footwear business — a business where seasonal drops, deep catalogs, and paid channels that all claim the same sale make clean measurement genuinely hard. Your event profile points at content marketing & SEO and product discovery & personalization — so that's what this page is built around. Triple Whale brings retention and lifecycle data into the same measurement layer as everything else, and Moby acts like an extra analyst on your lifecycle team: watching segments, drafting ideas, and never sending anything without your approval.
pts: Email and SMS performance only makes sense next to the rest of the business. When lifecycle revenue sits in the same view as paid and site data, you can prove retention's contribution instead of asserting it.
Segmentation is where lifecycle programs win or stall. Customer-level data — repeat rates, time between orders, product affinities — should drive who gets which message.
Post-purchase experience is a retention lever. Measuring it alongside lifecycle revenue shows which fixes actually move second-purchase rate.

wf.m: Moby monitors repeat rate, time-to-second-purchase, and revenue by segment and flow.
wf.d: It drafts a weekly digest: which flows and segments are gaining or fading, and why.
wf.a: You approve any changes to flows or campaigns — Moby never sends on its own.

wf.m: Custom dashboards connect content, social, and influencer activity to revenue and customer quality.
wf.d: Moby drafts the monthly story for leadership — what worked, what to repeat, what to retire.
wf.a: You approve and present it; the analysis hours come back to your team.

wf.m: Moby watches on-site search, PDP engagement, and discovery paths against revenue per session.
wf.d: It drafts which discovery improvements actually paid for themselves — in revenue, not engagement metrics.
wf.a: You approve the roadmap order.

katherine-martinez-rent-the-runway.hook: Maxx's 9:35am session on first-party data and profit maps directly to lifecycle work: the data is yours; the profit is the retention curve.
